articles.title: Wild yeast discovery enables non-GM brewing of ornithine-enriched craft beer
articles.summary: As consumer interest grows in foods and beverages with added nutritional value, brewers are exploring ways to improve fermentation itself rather than relying on post-production additives. Ornithine, a naturally occurring amino acid involved in several biological processes, has attracted attention as a promising ingredient for value-added products. However, increasing ornithine production in brewing yeast is difficult because the metabolic pathway is tightly regulated, making conventional improvement strategies challenging.

articles.title: Trained AI outperforms biologists at spotting salmon lice
articles.summary: Researchers have taken over 120,000 images of salmon lice larvae in seawater and used them to train AI models. The models were much faster and more accurate than experienced biologists at identifying the parasites that feed on the skin and blood of salmonids.

articles.title: Small-molecule switches put therapeutic CRISPR editing under on-demand control in living tissues
articles.summary: In a study published in Science Translational Medicine, a team of researchers led by Dr. Wang Yu from the Shenzhen Institutes of Advanced Technology of the Chinese Academy of Sciences developed PRINCE and Little Prince, dual small-molecule-controlled genome editing systems that allow CRISPR activity to be switched on by drug inducers and kept largely silent in their absence.

articles.title: Giant wheat starch granules—a leap forward in biological engineering with potential benefits for diet, manufacturing
articles.summary: Scientists have grown wheat containing supersized starch granules—a leap forward in biological engineering with potential benefits for our daily diets and a raft of industrial applications.

articles.title: How a new fungal genome-editing tool could open fresh paths to cancer treatments
articles.summary: Researchers have spent decades—and billions of dollars—sequencing animal and crop genomes, but fungi have historically been the forgotten middle child of genomics, only noticed when they're ruining bread or colonizing toes.

articles.title: Author Correction: Improved RNA base editing with guide RNAs mimicking highly edited endogenous ADAR substrates
articles.summary: Nature Biotechnology, Published online: 03 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s41587-026-03246-6 Author Correction: Improved RNA base editing with guide RNAs mimicking highly edited endogenous ADAR substrates

articles.title: How to stop a mouse plague
articles.summary: The scenes are biblical. Tens of thousands of rodents scattering across canola fields, behind sheds, into machinery. River fish with bellies full of mice. Carcasses littering the street, the sidewalk, outside your home. In supermarkets, the inescapable stench of dead and dying mice. These are the undeniable signs of a mouse plague.

articles.title: A WRAP for biology's greasiest problem
articles.summary: Embedded in the boundary between the inside and outside of each cell are membrane proteins. They act as first responders by sensing signals, regulating which molecules enter and leave the cell, and enabling cells to quickly adapt to changes in their environment.

articles.title: Rethinking the governance of human embryo research: Comparing Japan's guidelines with international standards
articles.summary: Human embryo models can help researchers study early human development and infertility without relying solely on human embryos. As the technology advances, these models are becoming more complex and can be maintained in culture for longer periods. These developments have raised new questions about how such research should be regulated.

articles.title: AI could bring satellite crop monitoring to the world's most vulnerable farms
articles.summary: Small farms grow much of the world's food, but from space they are nearly invisible. Their fields are tiny and ill-defined, and the satellite tools built to track crops were designed for the large, uniform fields of industrial agriculture, not the sub-hectare plots that feed many of the world's poorest people.

articles.title: Binance outflows triple to $1.2B as ETH withdrawals hit 3-year high
articles.summary: Binance recorded $1.23 billion in weekly net outflows, up 207% from the previous week, as Ethereum withdrawals surged to a three-year high.

articles.title: Barstool's Portnoy plans to hold bitcoin down to zero after timing it wrong every time
articles.summary: Barstool Sports founder Dave Portnoy said he will hold bitcoin all the way down to zero after buying near $100,000.

articles.title: Vitalik Buterin shares top priorities for new 'Lean Ethereum' strawmap
articles.summary: Part of the Ethereum Foundation’s plan to make Ethereum more private and scalable is to introduce a new virtual machine, with leanISA and RISC-V among the top candidates.

articles.title: Moonbeam to pivot from Polkadot to Base, unveils AI agent framework
articles.summary: Moonbeam didn’t provide a timeline for when it would launch the AI agent platform but told GLMR holders to bridge their tokens from the Polkadot parachain to Base before July 31.

articles.title: Bitcoin ETFs log record eighth straight negative week despite large Thursday inflow
articles.summary: Hyperliquid ETFs took in $4.3 million, their smallest week since launching in May, down from a record $111 million the week before.

articles.title: Nearly 1 million wallets are down $3.81 billion on Trump’s memecoin: report
articles.summary: Trump's annual financial disclosure showed a $636 million payout tied to the token and more than $1.4 billion in total crypto-related income for 2025.

articles.title: Tokenization's next use case is personalized portfolios, NYLIM executive says
articles.summary: Thomas Sy, head of multi-asset solutions at the $800-million asset manager, says blockchain can enable complex portfolio construction that's not yet possible in traditional finance.

articles.title: Kraken lets traders use tokenized stocks as collateral for leveraged trades
articles.summary: Eligible users can now use select tokenized stocks and ETFs as collateral for futures and margin trading without selling their holdings.

articles.title: Bitcoin jumps above $63,000, reversing end-June losses
articles.summary: The token hit its highest in over a month during thin July 4 trading, with XRP up 5% in 24 hours to lead gains among majors.
